# Autogenerated data triage

dtk emits **199 `auto_generated` data objects** (24,850 B) for retail address
ranges that `splits.txt` assigns to no translation unit. **Every one scores
`fuzzy_match_percent == 100`** and every one is excluded from the scored `game`/`sdk`
categories, so the class is invisible to the tracked metrics in both directions.

Regenerate with `python3 tools/autogen_data_triage.py` after any rebuild of
`report.json`. Companion manifest: [`autogen_data_triage.csv`](autogen_data_triage.csv).

## Verdict: real content, but not claimable headroom

These spans are **not** padding and **not** a reporting artifact - they are genuine retail
data belonging to TUs we already have. But claiming them is blocked twice over:

1. **Attribution lives in `splits.txt`, not `symbols.txt`.** An auto object exists precisely
   because no split claims its address range. Extending a split regenerates the target
   objects under `build/GSAE01/obj/`, which are READ-ONLY by project rule.
2. **Our source does not produce these bytes.** Most auto symbols are *pinned*: the source
   declares `extern f32 lbl_803DE7C0;` and relocates against retail's address rather than
   defining the constant, so our TU emits nothing to compare. Claiming the range would
   expose a short section and **lower** the score.

The empirical check: of the auto `.sdata2` spans referenced by exactly **one** target unit,
our `.o`'s `.sdata2` bytes match the retail span in **1 case out of 42** - elsewhere ours is
typically 4-8x smaller (just the `43300000`/`80000000` int-to-double magic pair).

Converting the pins into real definitions is the known **pool defs-vs-anons** axis, which
`.sdata2`'s positional all-or-nothing scoring makes a per-unit gate-crack, not a sweep.

## Where the bytes are, by symbol class

The load-bearing axis: does anything in *our* tree already name the symbol?

| symbol class | bytes | % | what it means |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `src-pinned` | 19,987 | 80.4% | a `.c`/`.h` in this tree names the symbol, almost always as a dangling `extern` pin so the code relocates against retail's address and our TU emits no bytes for it |
| `symtxt-only` | 4,077 | 16.4% | named in `symbols.txt`, referenced by retail code, never spelled in our source - our TU emits the value as an anonymous pool/literal instead |
| `pad` | 388 | 1.6% | dtk alignment filler (`pad_*` symbols); no original C object |
| `orphan` | 80 | 0.3% | neither source nor `symbols.txt` mentions it, and nothing relocates against it |
| **total** | 24,850 | 100% | |

## By section

| section | objs | bytes | note |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `.sdata2` | 113 | 15,380 | MWCC read-only constant pools - the dominant class, and the hard one |
| `.bss` | 21 | 3,576 | zero-init; no bytes exist in the DOL to match |
| `.data` | 7 | 3,176 | mostly dead debug string blobs (see below) |
| `.sbss` | 34 | 1,928 | zero-init small BSS |
| `.sdata` | 23 | 734 | small initialised constants |
| `.sbss2` | 1 | 56 | zero-init small BSS |

## Who references these spans

Reverse map built from relocations in the read-only target objects against the symbols each
auto object defines. This identifies the true owning TU far more reliably than the
address-adjacency guess in the CSV's `adjacent_owner` column.

- **66 spans** are referenced by exactly one target unit - an unambiguous owner.
- **128 spans** are referenced by several: dtk merged a contiguous run covering
  the pool contributions of several consecutive TUs into one object, because none of them
  carries a split for that section.
- **5 spans** (732 B) are referenced by nothing at
  all - dead debug strings and dtk padding.

## The `.data` spans are dead debug strings

All seven are string blobs (or small tables beside them) for `OSReport`-style diagnostics
whose call sites the retail build compiled out: Tricky walk-group traces
(`tricky wg %d->%d target wg %d`), `GUARD_INIT`/`GUARD_FINDING` state names,
`!!!!!!!!!!! TRIGGER %d  ident %d`, and one 440 B block of GX hang diagnostics
(`Suspected graphics hang or infinite loop`) that nothing in the DOL references.
Recovering them means writing the debug code that printed them - out of scope.

## The unscored-output corollary

The mirror image of this gap is worth knowing: **153 of our source units emit a `.sdata2`
section (3,428 B total) whose target object has no `.sdata2` at all.** Those bytes are
compared against nothing, so they can be arbitrarily wrong and no gate will say so. Same
blind-spot family as unresolved relocation targets.

## Score arithmetic

`game` data today: 1,122,364/1,140,572 = 98.40%. Folding all 24,850 auto bytes into
that denominator with today's source would give 1,122,364/1,165,422 = 96.31%, i.e. **2.10 pp worse**.
Attribution is only a win once the bytes are also produced.

## Method (reproducible)

`tools/autogen_data_triage.py`. Per object: `powerpc-eabi-objdump -h -r -s -t` on the
read-only target `.o` for byte signals (Shannon entropy, `0xFF`-padding fraction, relocation
density, pointer-word fraction, printable-ASCII fraction) and for its symbol table; symbols
are then classified against every identifier in `src/` + `include/` and against
`symbols.txt`. Referencing units come from a full relocation scan of every non-auto target
object. No target object and no split is modified.
